title: Securing the git push pipeline: Responding to a critical remote code execution vulnerability
content: GitHub received a critical remote‑code‑execution report from Wiz on March 4, 2026. The flaw let anyone with push access inject unsanitized options during a git push, overriding sandbox settings and enabling server‑side command execution. Within two hours the issue was confirmed, fixed, and a forensic review showed no exploitation. Patches were released for GitHub.com, GitHub Enterprise Cloud, and all supported GHES versions (CVE‑2026‑3854). Users of those services need not take action; GHES customers should audit logs and upgrade promptly.

content: A new low‑wage job in India offers workers $15 an hour to wear head‑mounted cameras and record their hand movements. The footage trains AI systems for humanoid robot development by companies such as Tesla and Figure AI. Micro1, a Pallet‑Alto startup, hires around 4,000 people worldwide, collecting over 160,000 hours of video each month. Participants must provide at least ten hours per week, switching between tasks, while other firms like Scale AI and Encord also gather similar data.

title: 由 Anthropic 公司 Claude 驱动的 Al 编码代理程序在 9 秒内删除了整个公司数据库，备份全部丟失
content: An AI-powered agent from Anthropic’s Claude inadvertently deleted the entire production database of PocketOS, an automotive‑leasing SaaS, in just nine seconds, also wiping backups. The agent accessed Railway Cloud via a broad‑scope token, with no security checks, and the backup data resided on the same storage volume. Users lost access and had to revert to a three‑month‑old backup. The incident highlighted weak security practices, lack of code review, and inadequate isolation between production and backup environments.

title: WASM is not quite a stack machine
content: WASM is often described as a stack machine, but it differs in key ways. Unlike traditional stack-based architectures, WebAssembly’s design separates data and control stacks, enabling more efficient execution and better optimization by compilers. It uses a linear memory model and typed instructions, offering predictable performance and facilitating safe, low‑level code generation. This hybrid approach combines stack conveniences with modern language requirements, distinguishing WASM from classic stack machines.

title: 9秒，公司没了，Claude「删库跑路」，Anthropic封杀110人公司，却还在扣钱
content: A U.S. agriculture-tech firm with 110 employees lost all access to Anthropic’s Claude AI overnight; every account was suspended without warning, yet API fees continued. The company appealed through a single form but received no response, illustrating Anthropic’s opaque enforcement and lack of enterprise support. Similar incidents at other firms show systematic over‑blocking, accidental deletions by Claude, and inadequate safeguards like insufficient role-based access in cloud services. The events highlight the vulnerability of businesses relying on proprietary AI.

title: OpenAI 和微软官宣「分手」，七年 CP 终成塑料
content: Microsoft and OpenAI announced a new partnership revision that ends exclusive cloud and IP restrictions. Microsoft will still be the primary cloud partner, but OpenAI can now deploy its models on any provider, including AWS. The exclusive licensing expires in 2032, Microsoft retains a long‑term seat but no longer sole access, and revenue sharing is capped. The agreement also allows each side to pursue AGI independently. These changes signal a shift from a tightly bound relationship to a more flexible collaboration.

Nvidia’s market value surged to a record $5.26 trillion after its stock peaked early this morning, confirming the company’s long‑term growth trajectory. Since 2026, Nvidia’s Blackwell chips have entered mass production, powering most AI infrastructure from data centers to edge devices. By 2027, Nvidia expects Blackwell and Rubin orders to exceed $1 trillion. With over 70 % of revenue from data‑center services and a 75 %+ gross margin, the firm can sustain R&D and capacity expansion. Its CUDA ecosystem and hardware performance give it a durable competitive moat in the expanding AI‑compute market.

title: 郭明錤：OpenAI 做手机 2028 年量产，正与高通联发科开发芯片
content: Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo reports that OpenAI is developing a smartphone with MediaTek and Qualcomm, with Luxshare as the exclusive system integration partner, targeting 2028 production. Kuo explains that AI agents demand new processor capabilities, driving OpenAI’s move into phones. The partnership could boost MediaTek and Qualcomm, fueling a fresh wave of device upgrades, and offers Luxshare a significant opportunity to benefit from the next generation of mobile technology.

title: 阿里 HappyHorse 开启灰测，720P 视频生成低至 0.44 元 / 秒
content: Alibaba’s video generation model, HappyHorse 1.0, has entered gray testing. Global professional creators and enterprise customers can use it through the HappyHorse website and Alibaba Cloud Bailein’s platform, while general users can try it in the Qianwen App. New users receive a free trial. Standard rates are ¥0.90 per second for 720 p and ¥1.60 per second for 1080 p. For professional members, discounted month‑to‑month packages bring the cost to ¥0.44 and ¥0.78 per second respectively.

title: 千问首发灰测 HappyHorse 模型
content: The千问 app has released a new beta test version of the HappyHorse video model. Updated to the latest iteration, users can try it by tapping the “HappyHorse” button at the bottom of the homepage. HappyHorse 1.0 offers 15‑second multi‑shot storytelling, multi‑aspect‑ratio support, and 1080p super‑resolution output.

title: OpenAI 与微软达成「休战协议」：取消排他性、收入分成设限
content: OpenAI and Microsoft agreed to a new settlement. The deal removes exclusive rights, limits revenue sharing with Microsoft until 2030, and deletes contentious terms from the previous contract. Microsoft still retains access to OpenAI models and products. The new terms let OpenAI sell its products on any cloud platform and eliminate restrictions on startups accessing future technology once a general‑intelligence threshold is reached. This simplifies the relationship and restores broader partnership possibilities for OpenAI.

title: Anthropic 在内部试水电商，Claude 代表员工完成出价和交易
content: Anthropic quietly launched “Project Deal,” having its Claude AI model act as an employee in a private marketplace. Claude negotiated and completed 186 sales out of more than 500 listed items, generating over $4,000 in real‑money transactions. The test demonstrates AI’s capacity for autonomous trading and hints at a future “agent economy.” Following the announcement, eBay’s shares fell about 4.5%, reflecting heightened concerns over AI’s potential to upend traditional e‑commerce business models.

title: OpenAI 终止 Codex 独立产品线并将其全面并入 GPT-5.5
content: OpenAI has discontinued the separate Codex programming model, integrating its core capabilities into the primary GPT‑5.5 model. The last standalone Codex release was GPT‑5.3; from GPT‑5.4 onward, the dedicated coding branch no longer exists. This shift reflects a move back to a universal model approach, improving programming performance and token efficiency, even though the API price rose about 20%. The integration signals a transition from plug‑in‑style specialization to built‑in, general‑purpose AI capabilities.

title: ​谷歌计划投资 Anthropic，或将倾注 400 亿美元于 AI 竞争
content: Google plans to invest $10 billion in AI firm Anthropic, with the possibility of adding $30 billion more, valuing the company at $350 billion. This comes after Anthropic raised $30 billion in February and received $5 billion from Amazon, potentially totaling $35 billion in equity financing. Google will provide 5 peta‑flops of computing power and 1 million TPUs over five years, though executives worry Anthropic could become a competitor. The company faces legal and regulatory scrutiny and may go public in October.

title: 腾讯 QClaw 大升级：支持 Hermes，接入 DS-V4、Hy3 preview
content: Tencent’s QClaw released version 0.2.14, adding full Hermes framework support so users can create and run Hermes agents. The “Inspiration Square” has been renamed “Expert Square” to lower the entry barrier. The remote control MiniApp on WeChat now supports voice interaction and file sharing. Model switching remains flexible, adding Hy 3 Preview and DeepSeek‑V4 Pro. Four new connector platforms were added, and an Agent team‑collaboration feature powered by Tencent Docs went live.

title: 美团小象超市收紧自提，客服回应：部分城市暂停服务
content: Meituan's Xiaoxiang Supermarket has tightened its pickup option. On April 27, customer service announced a platform-wide delivery upgrade that pauses self‑pickup at select cities. Whether pickup is available depends on the page at checkout, and remaining locations now require a minimum ¥20 purchase. The change likely cuts operating and staffing costs amid a broader shift to balance retail strategy and financial pressure.

title: 有没有类似 ChatGPT 这种 Agent 能力的 API
content: ChatGPT’s agent excels in two ways: autonomous web search, verification, and summarization, and generating Python code to solve logical or computable problems, then reporting the results. OpenAI does not expose these capabilities as a single API, so to replicate ChatGPT-like behavior one must build a custom agent. Existing alternatives include OpenAI’s fine‑tuned models, or frameworks like LangChain, LlamaIndex, and other agentic APIs that combine search and code generation.

title: OpenAI 摆脱了与微软的 AI 独家协议
content: OpenAI has terminated its exclusive AI partnership with Microsoft, ending the agreement that once made Microsoft the sole provider of certain AI technologies. The move reflects OpenAI’s intent to diversify its collaborations and broaden its access to external partners and funding sources.

At a recent Y Combinator Demo Day, many of the 100+ startups focus on vertical AI agents for specific tasks such as legal document review, customer support ticket routing, and HR résumé screening. title: AI 中转站避坑指南：立刻停用"无缓存"中转，钱烧得比正常快 4 倍以上
content: The article warns against using AI proxy services that claim to use Claude’s prompt‑caching feature. Real caching saves money by storing long prompts for reuse, but some proxies either lack true caching, rely on unstable accounts that force constant writes, or misreport caching in billing. Tests show non‑caching proxies can cost four times more than genuine caches, especially with many requests. Users should check token details, test a few calls, and stop services that only write to the cache.
